skill.name: wechat-article-search-api-skill
skill.description: This skill provides automated WeChat article extraction through the BrowserAct API. It enables keyword-based search, full article content retrieval, and structured data extraction from WeChat public accounts without manual scraping.
skill.summary: Extract full WeChat article content via BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. Static scanner flagged 52 potential issues, but after semantic evaluation, all findings are false positives or misclassifications. The skill makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct service with proper authentication (Bearer token). API key is read from environment variable (secure pattern). No command injection vectors, no prompt injection detected, no data exfiltration patterns. Risk factors (network, env_access, external_commands) are standard for legitimate API integration skills and do not constitute security concerns when properly implemented.

low_findings.title: Network Access via HTTP Libraries
low_findings.description: Script uses Python requests library to make HTTP calls to BrowserAct API. This is legitimate API integration with no exfiltration patterns. Requests are authenticated with Bearer token to known service endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear legitimate API usage - requests library used to call BrowserAct API for WeChat article search. Bearer token authentication present. No suspicious endpoints or data exfiltration patterns.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
low_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variable using os.getenv(). This is the secure pattern for credential management - no hardcoded secrets present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of secure credential handling - API key read from environment, not hardcoded. Follows security best practices.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoint URL
low_findings.description: API_BASE_URL is hardcoded to browseract.com endpoint. This is expected configuration - not a security issue as the service endpoint is legitimate and known.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate service endpoint configuration. No malicious destination detected.

low_findings.title: Bash Command Invocation in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains example bash command showing script invocation. This is documentation, not actual command execution. No user input injection possible.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation example only. Script parameters are passed as positional arguments with no shell expansion or injection risk.

content.user_title: Search and extract full WeChat articles via API
content.value_statement: Extracting WeChat article content manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates WeChat article search and full content extraction through the BrowserAct API, enabling social listening and market research workflows.
actual_capabilities: Search WeChat articles by keywords using BrowserAct API
Extract full article content including body, author, publication date, and images
Monitor brand mentions and industry trends across WeChat public accounts
Retrieve structured article data for analysis and storage
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (no free tier)
Rate limited by BrowserAct API quotas
Cannot access private or paywalled WeChat content
Execution time varies based on article complexity and server load

use_cases.title: Media monitoring and brand tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor how your brand or industry is discussed across WeChat public accounts. Track mentions, sentiment, and emerging trends in real-time.

use_cases.title: Competitive intelligence research
use_cases.description: Analyze competitor activities and market positioning by extracting full articles from relevant WeChat accounts. Build comprehensive competitor reports.

use_cases.title: Content curation for knowledge bases
use_cases.description: Automatically extract and archive valuable WeChat articles for internal knowledge bases. Build organized repositories of industry insights.

prompt_templates.title: Simple article search
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic keyword search with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Industry trend analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent WeChat articles about {industry} trends published in the last week

prompt_templates.title: Brand monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all WeChat articles mentioning {brand_name} from the past month

prompt_templates.title: Multi-keyword research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search WeChat for articles covering {keyword1} and {keyword2}. Get up to {limit} results
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced search with multiple keywords and result limits

output_examples.input: Search WeChat for articles about AI agents
output_examples.output: Found 5 articles. Top result: 'The Rise of AI Agents in Enterprise' by TechWeekly (published 2026-04-25). This article discusses how AI agents are transforming workplace productivity. [Full content extracted - 2500 words]

output_examples.input: Find content about smartphone market trends from April 2026
output_examples.output: Retrieved 3 articles analyzing Q1 2026 smartphone market data. Key insight: Android phone sales grew 15% while iPhone sales declined. Full analysis available in extracted content.

best_practices: Start with small extraction limits (5-10) to test before scaling up
Monitor API usage to stay within BrowserAct rate limits
Store extracted content with original URLs for citation and verification
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill without configuring the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Avoid requesting large batches (100+) in a single call - use pagination instead
Do not assume extracted content is current - WeChat articles may be deleted by authors

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why do I need an API key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a browser automation service that provides the WeChat search API. You need an API key to authenticate your requests and access the service. Get your key from browseract.com.

faq.question: Can this skill extract content from private WeChat accounts?
faq.answer: No. The skill only accesses publicly available WeChat content. Private accounts and paywalled articles are not accessible.

faq.question: How long does article extraction take?
faq.answer: Execution time varies from 30 seconds to several minutes depending on the number of articles requested and server load. The script provides status updates during execution.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The skill will return an 'Invalid authorization' error and will not retry. You must provide a valid API key to continue.

faq.question: Can I extract articles from a specific WeChat account?
faq.answer: Yes. You can search using the account name as keywords. However, search accuracy depends on how the account is indexed in WeChat.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many articles I can extract?
faq.answer: BrowserAct imposes rate limits. Start with smaller batches (5-10) and scale up based on your API quota. The script supports setting extraction limits via parameters.
